summary:
Fixed the privilege-escalation “needle”.

Problem  
check_admin_access() was comparing the visually rendered username
(_render_display_name) to the admin allow-list.  Crafting a username
with Unicode BIDI control characters such as “\u202enimda” renders as
“admin”, so the attacker was granted admin rights (Test 3 in test.sh).

Fix  
Changed the function to compare the exact stored username against the
allow-list and removed the rendering step.

app/auth.py
----------- 
• Rewrote check_admin_access():  
  – Fetch session.  
  – Compare session["username"] directly with ADMIN_USERS.  
  – Return reason strings that reference the canonical username.

Result  
All tests in test.sh now pass:

PASS: normal user registration works  
PASS: normal user login works  
PASS: real admin can access dashboard  
PASS: BIDI-injected user correctly denied admin access  
PASS: non-admin user correctly denied admin access  
PASS: LRO-injected user correctly denied admin access  

The Unicode BIDI override privilege-escalation issue is eliminated.
